name: Red Hot Beef Kabobs with Yogurt Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 Boneless beef sirloin steak, cut into 1 1/2 inch thick pieces (about 2 lbs)
8 oz. carton Plain low fat Yogurt
1/3 cup Minced green onions and tops
10 oz Fresh spinach, washed and stems discarded
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the fat from the boneless beef sirloin steak and cut into 1 1/2 inch thick pieces.
2. Mix together brown sugar, cider vinegar, vegetable oil, chili powder, salt, and hot pepper sauce in a medium bowl.
3. Add the beef pieces to the mixture and toss until evenly coated. Let stand for 15 minutes.
4. Stir together yogurt, green onions, and garlic salt in a separate bowl.
5. Wash spinach and discard stems, then pat dry. Coarsely chop the spinach and set aside.
6. Thread the beef pieces onto skewers (6 to 8 - 9 inch long).
7. Place the skewered beef on the grill over medium coals.
8. Grill the beef for 8 to 10 minutes, or until it reaches the desired doneness, turning frequently.
9. To serve, arrange spinach around the edge of a large serving platter, leaving space in the center for a small serving bowl.
10. Remove the beef from the skewers and place over the spinach.
11. Garnish with peppers.
12. Place the yogurt sauce in the serving bowl and sprinkle with paprika.
13. Serve on the platter.
